text.title: microservices-design Failure Modes
text.summary: Failure modes observed when applying the microservices-design methodology. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Two services SELECT from the same orders table; one ALTER breaks both.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated DB as shared store.
antipattern.fix: Move ownership to a single service; expose reads via API or async event.

antipattern.symptom: payments package imports orders.models.Order; deploy lockstep returns.
antipattern.root-cause: Author shortcut to avoid an API call.
antipattern.fix: Define an OrderView in payments; populate via a contract call or async event.

antipattern.symptom: payments service slow; orders piles up requests; entire mesh stalls.
antipattern.fix: Wrap the call in CircuitBreaker with reasonable thresholds + timeout.

antipattern.symptom: A user reports a 30s checkout; team has no distributed trace; root cause hunt takes days.
antipattern.fix: Add tracing middleware + propagate trace IDs; ship a Jaeger/Tempo backend.

antipattern.symptom: orders v1 contract changed; payments deploys break.
antipattern.fix: Add /v1 in URL or v1 in proto package; support old version >= N days.
